name: Posture & Prompt Generation
description: System prompt generation for each phase and posture combination. All prompts are string constants or f-string templates defined inline (no external files). Provides phase-specific system prompts for understand, challenge, and synthesize phases, plus a revision prompt. Includes posture selection logic with random default and environment variable override. Dependency direction: posture.py imports from models.py only.

types.name: Phase
types.description: Conversation phase determining which system prompt skeleton to use. Defined in models.py, referenced here.

types.name: Posture
types.description: Adversarial posture for the challenge phase. Each posture defines a distinct critical lens. Defined in models.py, referenced here.

types.name: ProblemModel

fields.name: system_description
fields.description: What the system does at a high level

fields.name: primary_goal
fields.description: The main objective or problem to solve

fields.name: stakes
fields.description: What happens if the solution fails or is wrong

fields.name: history
fields.description: Prior attempts, existing solutions, relevant context

fields.name: dependencies
fields.description: External systems, libraries, APIs the solution depends on

fields.name: assumptions
fields.description: Things assumed to be true but not verified

fields.name: challenged_assumptions
fields.description: Assumptions that were questioned during challenge phase

fields.name: risk_areas
fields.description: Identified risk areas from challenge phase

types.description: Structured representation of the engineer's problem as understood so far. Defined in models.py as a Pydantic BaseModel with JSON serialization. Fields accumulate across understand and challenge phases.

types.name: ChallengedAssumptionList
types.description: List of assumptions that were challenged and their resolutions

types.name: RiskAreaList
types.description: List of identified risk area descriptions

types.name: PostureDescription

fields.name: description
fields.description: Human-readable description of what this posture does

types.description: A posture paired with its human-readable description string for display purposes.

types.name: PostureDescriptionMap

fields.name: ADVERSARIAL
fields.description: Description for adversarial posture: actively tries to break the solution

fields.name: CONTRARIAN
fields.description: Description for contrarian posture: argues the opposite of stated positions

fields.name: CRITIC
fields.description: Description for critic posture: evaluates quality and completeness rigorously

fields.name: SKEPTIC
fields.description: Description for skeptic posture: questions evidence and certainty levels

fields.name: COLLABORATOR
fields.description: Description for collaborator posture: constructively identifies gaps and alternatives

types.description: Module-level constant POSTURE_DESCRIPTIONS: dict[Posture, str]. Maps each Posture variant to its human-readable display description. All 5 postures must have entries.

functions.name: get_system_prompt
functions.description: Main dispatch function. Returns the complete system prompt string for the given phase. Delegates to phase-specific private builders (_understand_prompt, _challenge_prompt, _synthesize_prompt). The returned prompt includes the serialized problem model state and, for understand/challenge phases, the structured JSON block output instruction.

inputs.name: phase
inputs.description: The current conversation phase determining prompt skeleton selection

inputs.name: problem_model
inputs.description: Current accumulated problem model state, serialized into prompt as context

inputs.name: posture
inputs.description: Required for CHALLENGE phase. Selects which adversarial lens to apply. Ignored for UNDERSTAND and SYNTHESIZE phases.

error_cases.name: missing_posture_for_challenge
error_cases.condition: phase is CHALLENGE and posture is None

error_data.message: Posture is required for CHALLENGE phase

error_cases.name: unknown_phase
error_cases.condition: phase is not a recognized Phase enum value

preconditions: phase is a valid Phase enum value
problem_model is a valid ProblemModel instance (may have empty/default fields)
If phase is CHALLENGE, posture must not be None
postconditions: Returned string is non-empty
Returned string contains a serialized representation of the problem model
For UNDERSTAND phase: returned string contains collaborative interviewer instructions and JSON block output format specification
For CHALLENGE phase: returned string contains the posture-specific adversarial lens instructions and JSON block output format specification
For SYNTHESIZE phase: returned string contains prompt.md and constraints.yaml format specifications without JSON block instruction
Returned string uses no external file references — all content is inline

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total size of problem model fields

functions.name: get_revision_prompt
functions.description: Returns the system prompt for the single revision cycle. Instructs the LLM to regenerate prompt.md and constraints.yaml artifacts based on the engineer's feedback. Includes the full problem model and the engineer's feedback text.

inputs.name: feedback
inputs.description: The engineer's feedback on the initial synthesis artifacts, describing what to change or improve

inputs.name: problem_model
inputs.description: The final accumulated problem model state from understand and challenge phases

error_cases.name: empty_feedback
error_cases.condition: feedback is an empty string or only whitespace

preconditions: problem_model is a valid ProblemModel instance representing the completed understand+challenge state
postconditions: Returned string is non-empty
Returned string contains the engineer's feedback text verbatim
Returned string contains a serialized representation of the problem model
Returned string contains prompt.md and constraints.yaml output format specifications
Returned string instructs regeneration (not incremental patching) of artifacts

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total size of problem model fields + feedback length

functions.name: select_posture
functions.description: Selects a challenge posture using a three-tier priority: (1) explicit override parameter, (2) CONSTRAIN_POSTURE environment variable, (3) random.choice from all Posture variants. Used by the engine before entering the challenge phase to determine which adversarial lens to apply.

inputs.name: override
inputs.description: Explicit posture override. Highest priority. When provided, returned directly without consulting env var or random selection.

error_cases.name: invalid_env_posture
error_cases.condition: override is None and CONSTRAIN_POSTURE environment variable is set but its value does not match any Posture variant name (case-insensitive)

error_data.message: Invalid CONSTRAIN_POSTURE value: '{value}'. Must be one of: ADVERSARIAL, CONTRARIAN, CRITIC, SKEPTIC, COLLABORATOR

postconditions: Returned value is a valid Posture enum member
If override is not None, returned value equals override
If override is None and CONSTRAIN_POSTURE env var is set to a valid posture name, returned value matches the env var
If override is None and CONSTRAIN_POSTURE env var is not set, returned value is one of the 5 Posture variants (selected randomly)
side_effects: Uses random.choice when no override or env var is set (non-deterministic)

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the CONSTRAIN_POSTURE env var as a posture override for testing/deterministic selection

functions.name: get_posture_description
functions.description: Returns a human-readable description of the given posture for display to the engineer. Looks up the posture in the POSTURE_DESCRIPTIONS constant dict.
preconditions: posture is a valid Posture enum member
postconditions: Returned string is non-empty
Returned string is a human-readable sentence or short paragraph describing the posture's behavior

functions.name: _format_problem_model
functions.description: Private helper. Serializes a ProblemModel instance into a human-readable text block suitable for inclusion in system prompts. Renders each field with a label, omitting fields that are empty or at their default values. Used by all prompt builder functions.
preconditions: model is a valid ProblemModel instance
postconditions: Returned string contains only fields from model that have non-default values
Each included field is rendered with a human-readable label and its value
List fields are rendered as bulleted sub-items
If all fields are at defaults, returns a string indicating no information gathered yet

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total size of all problem model fields

functions.name: _understand_prompt
functions.description: Private builder. Constructs the Phase 1 (Understand) system prompt. Instructs the LLM to act as a collaborative interviewer, provides the interview structure (system, stakes, history, dependencies, boundaries, assumptions), includes current problem model state, and appends the JSON block output instruction.

inputs.name: problem_model
inputs.description: Current problem model state for context

postconditions: Returned string contains collaborative interviewer role instruction
Returned string enumerates interview structure areas: system, stakes, history, dependencies, boundaries, assumptions
Returned string contains serialized problem model from _format_problem_model
Returned string ends with or contains the _JSON_BLOCK_INSTRUCTION for structured output

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is problem model size

functions.name: _challenge_prompt
functions.description: Private builder. Constructs the Phase 2 (Challenge) system prompt for a specific posture. Injects the posture-specific adversarial/contrarian/critic/skeptic/collaborator lens describing what to look for and how to frame challenges. Includes accumulated problem model and JSON block instruction.

inputs.name: posture
inputs.description: The specific adversarial posture to apply

preconditions: posture is a valid Posture enum member
postconditions: Returned string contains posture-specific challenge instructions matching the given posture
Returned string contains serialized problem model from _format_problem_model
Returned string contains the _JSON_BLOCK_INSTRUCTION for structured output
ADVERSARIAL posture prompt instructs to actively try to break the solution approach
CONTRARIAN posture prompt instructs to argue the opposite of stated positions
CRITIC posture prompt instructs to evaluate quality and completeness rigorously
SKEPTIC posture prompt instructs to question evidence and certainty levels
COLLABORATOR posture prompt instructs to constructively identify gaps and alternatives

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is problem model size

functions.name: _synthesize_prompt
functions.description: Private builder. Constructs the Phase 3 (Synthesize) system prompt. Instructs the LLM to generate prompt.md (briefing style) and constraints.yaml (structured YAML schema) from the full conversation history and problem model. Specifies exact output formats. Does NOT include the JSON block instruction (synthesis uses different output structure).
postconditions: Returned string contains instructions for generating prompt.md in briefing style
Returned string contains the YAML schema specification for constraints.yaml
Returned string contains serialized problem model from _format_problem_model
Returned string does NOT contain the _JSON_BLOCK_INSTRUCTION
Returned string specifies that output should contain both artifacts with clear delimiters

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is problem model size

invariants: All prompts are generated from inline string constants and templates — no external file reads
POSTURE_DESCRIPTIONS contains exactly 5 entries, one for each Posture variant
The _JSON_BLOCK_INSTRUCTION constant is included in understand and challenge prompts but never in synthesize or revision prompts
get_system_prompt dispatches to exactly one of _understand_prompt, _challenge_prompt, or _synthesize_prompt based on phase
posture.py imports only from models.py — no imports from cli.py, engine.py, session.py, or synthesizer.py
All public functions use type hints on all parameters and return types
String concatenation (not f-string interpolation) is used for JSON example blocks to avoid curly-brace escaping issues
select_posture priority order is always: parameter override > CONSTRAIN_POSTURE env var > random.choice
Every returned prompt string contains the serialized problem model state
Private functions and constants are prefixed with underscore
